steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main idea of the text. The passage describes Villasana's two parallel approaches — depicting cultural icons and depicting ordinary people — and ends with the unifying purpose: both show "the power and interconnectedness of all people." The main idea must capture both subject types and that shared purpose.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Villasana began her artistic career by painting portraits of famous people and then transitioned to depicting everyday people instead.': The passage presents both approaches as things she currently does — "in some works" and "in other works." There is no career progression described, and this choice implies she abandoned famous subjects. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Villasana's portrayal of a young girl sitting on a sidewalk focuses more on human connection than it does on human resilience.': The passage does not compare the weight of different themes within a single work. This judgment is invented. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Villasana's works focus on recognizing both famous and everyday examples of human strength and connection.': This names both categories of subject (famous and everyday) and reflects the passage's explicit statement that both approaches depict the power and interconnectedness of all people. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Villasana's depiction of Frida Kahlo receives more attention from scholars than her depiction of a young girl sitting on a sidewalk does.': The passage says nothing about scholarly attention to any work. This is entirely invented. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
